title: senecio repangae
intro: Senecio repangae, also known as Mount Victoria ragwort, is a rare, flowering plant native to New Zealand. It is a member of the daisy family and contains pyrrolizidine alkaloids, which have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties. These alkaloids have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing various health benefits, including the treatment of skin conditions and respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared as a tea or tincture, which are commonly used in herbal medicine, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using Senecio repangae due to its potential toxicity.
